message:
Simplify Notify poll cancel path and separate test assertions for token vs baton semantics

Remove the conditional baton-passing logic from Notified::poll that was
added in dbda2fcb. The previous approach checked was_notified and
notified_generation before deciding whether to call pass_baton() vs
drop(). This added complexity in both the optimistic (lock-free gen
check) and pessimistic (under-lock re-check) paths without correctly
handling all edge cases.

The simplified approach returns to unconditional drop(waiters) after
removing the waiter entry. Baton-passing for cancelled futures is
already handled at the correct layer by the Notify infrastructure's
existing Drop and wake mechanisms — the explicit conditional pass_baton
calls were redundant with that machinery and introduced subtle ordering
issues when the generation had already advanced.

Also refines the cancel-safety integration test to make the two
behavioral properties explicit with separate assertions:
- Polling a notified future to completion consumes the token (no
  leftover notification for the next future)
- Dropping a notified future that holds a pending notification must
  pass the baton to prevent lost wakeups

Previously the test used a single assert_eq comparing the two outcomes,
which conflated the distinct semantics of poll-consumption vs
drop-baton-passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
